brief.objective: introduce CALL as a leadership development and improvement planning tool
keyMessages: CALL provides a user-friendly school-wide needs assessment and feedback system for school improvement.
CALL supports professional growth, leadership development, and school improvement planning.
CALL focuses on leadership, improvement, and the work.
proofPoints: The CALL homepage describes CALL as a user-friendly school-wide needs assessment and feedback system.
The CALL site says CALL provides unique tools and customized services for professional growth, leadership development, and school improvement planning.
The What We Do page says CALL is school- and district-wide.
brief.layoutRecommendation: Use a one-page flyer with a strong headline, short audience framing, three concise support blocks, a source-grounded proof point, and one clear CTA.

headlineOptions: Focus on leadership. Focus on improvement. Focus on the work.
Leadership for learning, grounded in school-wide feedback
A practical system for professional growth and school improvement planning
subheadOptions: CALL helps principals use a school-wide leadership assessment and feedback system to support professional growth, leadership development, and school improvement planning.
The Comprehensive Assessment of Leadership for Learning provides practical, action-oriented data for leadership teams planning school improvement work.
Use CALL to gather multi-source feedback on core leadership practices distributed across your school building.
copy.body: CALL is a user-friendly school-wide needs assessment and feedback system for school improvement. Through a multi-source comprehensive survey, CALL assesses core leadership practices distributed across the school building and provides actionable formative feedback, including customized strategies and action plans for school improvement.
bullets: Support professional growth, leadership development, and school improvement planning.
Use practical and action-oriented data to guide inquiry and planning conversations.
Gather multi-source feedback on core leadership practices across the school building.
Connect feedback to customized strategies and action-based school improvement plans.

fittedCopy.headline: Focus on leadership. Focus on improvement. Focus on the work
fittedCopy.deck: Use leadership feedback to guide professional growth and school improvement planning.
proofPoints: Connect leadership development to school improvement planning.
Support administrators with tools and tailored services.

compositionTemplate.typographyScale: large campaign headline with compact support proof
compositionTemplate.artPlateTreatment: full-bleed branded atmosphere cropped into the composition
compositionTemplate.copyPlacement: left-led editorial stack with proof band integrated below

designRecipe.hierarchy: large campaign headline first, concise deck second, two readable proof points below, compact CTA anchored in the lower rhythm
designRecipe.artDirection: 4:5 CALL campaign flyer for school principals: an intentional editorial split with a calm left message field and a right-side visual story of instructional leadership, feedback, and school improvement planning expressed through authentic school leadership presence, purposeful movement, and subtle assessment-inspired graphic atmosphere
designRecipe.placeholderStrategy: use natural negative space, soft contrast, directional light, and crop to protect the left type zone; imply structure through composition only, with no placeholder boxes, fake copy, fake logos, or document panels
designRecipe.appComposition: app renders the official CALL logo at top-left, all headline/deck/proof/CTA text, and export; ImageGen shapes the full-bleed right-field art so it supports the left-led message and lower proof/CTA cadence
promptDirectives: Design the full flyer concept, not a decorative background.
Keep the left side quiet and readable for app-rendered typography.
Build the right-field story around leadership feedback guiding school improvement.
Use subtle branded assessment/learning atmosphere without fake text, logos, UI, or contact strips.

compositionScore.question: Would I send this to the WCEPS team without apology?

layoutQa.question: Would I send this layout to the WCEPS team without apology?

copyQualityQa.question: Would I send this copy to the WCEPS team without apology?
warnings: Generated copy repeats the same source idea across body, subhead, or bullets before copy-fit.

failureModes.message: Generated copy repeats the same source idea across body, subhead, or bullets before copy-fit.

visualQa.question: Would I send this visual system to the WCEPS team without apology?

renderQa.question: Would I send this rendered artifact to the WCEPS team without apology?

modelQa.question: Would a senior WCEPS reviewer send this artifact without apology?
warnings: Fitted copy is accurate but too generic for CALL; it does not clearly foreground CALL’s leadership-for-learning assessment, multi-source feedback, or school-wide needs-assessment value for principals.
“Support administrators with tools and tailored services” is broad, low-proof wording and risks sounding like scaffolded marketing copy rather than specific CALL evidence.
The final headline repeats “Focus on” three times; acceptable rhetorically, but it contributes to generic/monotonous campaign copy.
The image is clean and usable, but the visible scene could be any corporate planning meeting; school leadership, principal audience, and CALL feedback assessment are only weakly signaled.
The art plate reserves a very dark top-left area while render QA reports a native CALL logo variant; final logo contrast should be checked before export.

imageResults.revisedPrompt:
Create a 1024x1536 portrait full-bleed campaign art plate for an app-composed marketing flyer for CALL, aimed at school principals. This is a polished education-sector editorial split design comp, not a social post or contact card. The app will render the official logo, all typography, headline, proof copy, CTA, and export wrapper, so the image must contain absolutely no readable text, letters, numbers, labels, logos, wordmarks, icons, seals, badges, fake charts, UI, panels, cards, contact bars, or CTA-like elements.

Composition: intentional editorial split with a calm, quiet left-side message field reserved for app-rendered typography. Keep the left 45% of the canvas visually quiet, readable, and softly contrasting with subtle texture only. The right 55% should carry the visual story: a leadership-for-learning school improvement planning moment. Show an authentic principal and small leadership team in a reflective data-informed discussion, reviewing feedback reports and planning materials, but with all documents blurred/abstracted so no text, charts, labels, or numbers are readable. Avoid close-up faces and detailed hands; use natural body language, partial silhouettes, over-shoulder or mid-distance editorial crop.

Visual story: leadership feedback guiding school improvement; principal and leadership team planning; educators reviewing non-readable feedback materials; professional growth planning; steady, credible, calm school leadership atmosphere. Use props such as indistinct school improvement notes, abstract paper stacks, neutral folders, sticky-note-like shapes with no writing, and reflection artifacts, but no literal dashboard, fake UI, document layout, branded marks, labels, arrows, chevrons, or logos.

Style: authentic editorial photography or refined photo-illustrative realism with premium ad-agency quality, layered depth, rich visual density on the right, natural negative space on the left, soft contrast, directional window light, shallow depth of field, warm professional atmosphere. Color palette should subtly draw from deep navy #142836, teal #0081A4, green #5EA974, and warm orange #F2975E without forming logo-like marks or wallpaper patterns. Keep important visual energy away from trim and crop edges. No corporate boardroom, no generic PD workshop, no posed training-room stock, no random classroom stock, no fake dashboard, no template scaffolding, no washed-out empty fade. Full designed concept with hierarchy, visual rhythm, and type-safe contrast zones; text-free full-bleed campaign art plate only.

Model QA: “Support administrators with tools and tailored services” is broad, low-proof wording and risks sounding like scaffolded marketing copy rather than specific CALL evidence.
Model QA: The final headline repeats “Focus on” three times; acceptable rhetorically, but it contributes to generic/monotonous campaign copy.
Model QA: The image is clean and usable, but the visible scene could be any corporate planning meeting; school leadership, principal audience, and CALL feedback assessment are only weakly signaled.
Model QA: The art plate reserves a very dark top-left area while render QA reports a native CALL logo variant; final logo contrast should be checked before export.

request.goal: introduce CALL as a leadership development and improvement planning tool
request.topic: leadership for learning, professional growth, and school improvement planning
request.notes: Keep claims conservative because CALL copy must stay tightly source-bounded.

sourceEvidence.excerpt: CALL provides unique tools and customized services to support professional growth, leadership development, and school improvement planning.
sourceEvidence.label: CALL professional growth and school improvement

sourceEvidence.excerpt: Provides actionable formative feedback including customized strategies and action plans for school improvement.
sourceEvidence.label: Improvement-focused feedback

sourceEvidence.excerpt: CALL utilizes a multi-source comprehensive survey to assess core leadership practices that are distributed across the school building.
sourceEvidence.label: CALL multi-source assessment

sourceEvidence.excerpt: Data should be practical and action-oriented, and support for practitioners is best provided through inquiry-based coaching processes.
sourceEvidence.label: Practical, action-oriented data
